title: 顺水推舟
summary:
David Hunter denies to Rosalind that he has feelings for Lynne Marchmont, describing her as an arrogant and obnoxious girl. However, after leaving the room, David mutters to himself that Lynne's return will ruin their plans, revealing that he is intentionally taking paths to encounter her. Rosalind, meanwhile, expresses her superstitions to David, warning him of a mysterious stranger and a girl who will bring trouble.

Later, Adela Montgomery visits Rosalind to seek financial assistance. Adela is facing a crisis due to an overdrawn bank account, unpaid taxes, and house repair bills following the death of Gordon Croud. Adela had previously been hesitant to ask for help and was confused by Lynne's recent opposition to borrowing money from Mrs. Gordon. While waiting, Adela observes Rosalind's seemingly naive and childish demeanor, wondering if such behavior was an affectation that once attracted Gordon.

When Rosalind mentions David, Adela seizes the opportunity to ask for help. Despite her internal embarrassment and contempt for her own desperation, Adela explains her financial situation. Rosalind, appearing startled and innocent, offers to ask David for help, but Adela pushes for an immediate check. Rosalind locates a checkbook and writes a check for five hundred pounds. Adela accepts the check, which is written by Rosaline Croud, feeling a mixture of relief and contempt for Rosalind's perceived naivety.
